name: Oysters Randall
recipeInstructions:
In a medium saucepan, add frozen spinach and white wine. Cook covered over low heat until done. Cool and drain by squeezing spinach with hands or cheesecloth, reserving liquid.
In a medium saute pan, cook bacon until almost done, then add onions and garlic, continuing to cook until onions are golden. Remove the bacon mixture from the pan and add the cooking liquid from the spinach. Open the oysters (keeping one half-shell) and drain liquid into the pan as well. Add thyme and boil over medium heat until reduced to about 1/4 cup.
Add cream and hazelnut liqueur; continue cooking until the mixture is thickened and reduced a bit. Season with white pepper to taste.
In a medium mixing bowl, combine spinach with bacon mixture and cream sauce. Stir until thoroughly combined.
Top each oyster with 1-2 tablespoons of the spinach mixture. Sprinkle with Swiss cheese and an equal amount of Asiago cheese.
Roll puff pastry accordingly. Using one half-shell as a template, place on top of the pastry and cut out, then lift the piece of pastry and put it on top of the corresponding oyster. Continue this until all oysters are topped with pastry; discard any unused half-shells. Beat egg with 1 tablespoon water and brush tops of each oyster.
Place oysters on a baking sheet and bake in a 400-degree oven for 30-40 minutes or until the pastry is a dark golden brown. Serve immediately.
